symptoms: footprint/package substitution risk
part deletion risk

rootCause: Routing experiments can mutate BOM identity unless original spec is audited first.
fixSummary: Audit and lock original parts, footprints, pads, nets, board outline, and mounting holes before routing.
recipe: Compare derivative against original reference
Restore changed footprints/packages/pads/nets
Reject route runs if original spec is not verified

safetyRules: Do not rollback unrelated retained copper

symptoms: one failed net resets board
retained segments drop to zero

rootCause: Batch-level rollback erased valid routes when one candidate failed DRC.
fixSummary: Route one net transactionally, prove retained copper, and rollback only the failed candidate.
recipe: Scan retained copper before route
Write one route
Rescan KiCad file
Run local DRC
Rollback only failed net

safetyRules: Do not route current sense through switching copper
Do not route VBAT as thin signal trace

symptoms: routes crowd F.Cu/B.Cu
control nets near phase copper

rootCause: Router selected layers by convenience instead of ESC electrical role.
fixSummary: Use explicit 8-layer ESC layer roles for power, return, control, rails, and protected sense routing.
recipe: Classify net role
Select layer from ESC role policy
Reject sensitive control routes on In2.Cu unless justified

symptoms: via in keepout
via too close to pad
via-in-pad risk

rootCause: Via placement guessed sites without class-specific keepout and pad clearance checks.
fixSummary: Use standard through vias only, class by signal/power/stitching/sense, and reject illegal sites before writing.
recipe: Classify via class
Check pad/hole/edge/keepout clearance
Use via arrays for high current
Reject blind/buried/micro/via-in-pad

symptoms: floating generated track island
track stops near pad

rootCause: Route generator stopped near pad geometry while verifier correctly required copper contact.
fixSummary: Generate pad-edge contact and escape geometry that physically touches pad copper on the correct layer.
recipe: Extract pad copper geometry
Generate legal pad-edge contact
Stitch escape to main route
Reject floating generated copper

safetyRules: Do not call imported DRC fixed by hiding generated errors

symptoms: route commits then worsens clearance
candidate rolled back

rootCause: Route mutation did not classify generated DRC by exact family before selecting repair.
fixSummary: Classify generated DRC separately from imported debt, mutate only the route candidate, and keep other retained copper.
recipe: Run local DRC after candidate
Map DRC objects to generated route
Dogleg/shift/change layer/move via
Rollback only unrepaired candidate

symptoms: theoretical footprint replacement
missing stock proof

rootCause: Replacement flow could select parts without live stock or manual verification.
fixSummary: Gate replacements on exact MPN, supplier SKU, stock, lifecycle, ratings, pinout, and footprint compatibility.
recipe: Extract current MPN
Search supplier or import manual stock file
Verify ratings/pinout/footprint
Reject unverified stock

symptoms: bootstrap route retained but creates BOOT_SEL or switching-pad clearance errors

rootCause: Bootstrap/support routes can satisfy pad contact while still violating dense switching-node clearance against existing generated or pad copper.
fixSummary: Before promoting bootstrap routes, map generated DRC objects back to the candidate and require clean/repairable clearance against BOOT_SEL, SW pads, and nearby gate-drive copper.
recipe: Run local DRC after bootstrap candidate
Map clearance items to candidate net
Try alternate pad edge or dogleg
Rollback candidate if generated clearance remains

safetyRules: Do not count segments as success

symptoms: segments retained while KiCad unconnected count stays flat
copper drawn on a named net without resolving exact ratsnest item

rootCause: Router treated net labels as the work queue and committed copper without proving that the exact KiCad disconnected pad-to-pad item disappeared.
fixSummary: Use KiCad unconnected_items as the routing source of truth; route exact source/target endpoint pairs and commit only when the item disappears, unconnected count drops, island count drops, or KiCad confirms connectivity.
recipe: Run KiCad DRC and parse unconnected_items.
Map each unconnected item to exact source and target pads.
Route one exact ratsnest item transactionally.
Rerun KiCad DRC.
Commit only if that exact unconnected item disappears or net island/unconnected count decreases.
Rollback copper that adds segments without KiCad connectivity proof.

safetyRules: Do not count queue creation as routing
Do not count segments as success
Rollback candidates without KiCad connectivity proof

symptoms: KiCad unconnected items parsed
ratsnest queue populated
physical route attempt count remains zero

rootCause: BoardForge built the exact KiCad ratsnest work queue but stopped after audit/queue generation instead of passing queued endpoint pairs to the physical route generator.
fixSummary: The ratsnest queue must feed routeByExactUnconnectedItem and every queued item must end as committed, rolled back, precheck rejected, blocked with exact reason, or deferred with checkpoint.
recipe: Parse KiCad unconnected_items into exact endpoint pairs.
Pass queued endpoint pairs into the physical route generator.
Count precheck rejected, physically attempted, written, committed, rolled back, blocked, and resolved outcomes.
Reject QUEUED_NOT_ATTEMPTED as a final state unless a checkpoint is saved for runtime limit.

safetyRules: Do not rollback connecting routes before repair attempts

symptoms: route resolves exact ratsnest item
raw KiCad DRC worsens
candidate rolled back without local repair

rootCause: BoardForge rolled back routes that connected exact KiCad ratsnest items because generated DRC worsened, without attempting local route repair or mutation first.
fixSummary: If a route connects the exact KiCad ratsnest item, classify generated DRC and attempt dogleg/layer/via/clearance repair before rollback.
recipe: Write exact ratsnest candidate transactionally.
Verify exact unconnected item disappeared.
If DRC worsens, classify generated DRC instead of immediate rollback.
Try alternate layer, dogleg, via relocation/no-via, and clearance mutations.
Commit repaired route only if connectivity proof remains and DRC is not worse.

symptoms: exact ratsnest item connects transiently
candidate creates generated DRC
route is rolled back after failed generic repair

rootCause: BoardForge could temporarily connect exact KiCad ratsnest items, but the local repair loop did not classify generated DRC precisely enough to shove, dogleg, change layer, adjust width, or transactionally reroute blocking generated copper.
fixSummary: Run a local DRC-clean repair engine for connected candidates: classify generated DRC, apply net-role widths, dogleg/shove/change layer/move via, and transactionally reroute BoardForge-generated copper before final rollback.
recipe: Audit transiently connected candidates and classify generated DRC by family.
Select trace width from net role instead of using high-current defaults for signals.
Try dogleg, route shove, alternate layer, via relocation, and multi-segment bundles.
If generated copper blocks the route, transactionally reroute only generated copper and preserve original board spec.
Commit only if exact connectivity remains and generated DRC is repaired or improved.

safetyRules: Do not treat candidate generation as routing progress.
Do not keep routes without exact KiCad connectivity proof.
Do not change footprints, packages, board outline, mounting holes, pads, or nets.

symptoms: exact ratsnest item connects transiently
only a tiny number of doglegs/layer changes are tried
route is rolled back without an exhausted local search
segment count changes but exact KiCad connectivity does not improve

rootCause: BoardForge was smoke-testing routes instead of solving one exact ratsnest item to DRC-clean completion or mathematically proving the geometry was blocked.
fixSummary: For every exact ratsnest item, build a micro-route model, run clearance-aware pathfinding and local DRC repair with a real search budget, then commit only when KiCad connectivity is proven or return exact physical blocker geometry.
recipe: Select one exact KiCad unconnected item as the work unit.
Build a micro-route model from scanned KiCad pads, layers, tracks, vias, keepouts, and board outline.
Run clearance-aware obstacle inflation by net-role width and clearance before writing copper.
Search direct, dogleg, multi-segment, alternate-layer, and via-assisted paths with a real budget.
If a candidate connects but fails DRC, classify and locally repair it before rollback.
Commit only when exact KiCad connectivity is proven and generated DRC is clean or exactly reviewed.
If blocked, report exact layer/object/corridor geometry and the forbidden change required.

safetyRules: Do not modify PCB during backend detection

symptoms: Java missing
FreeRouting missing
DSN/SES workflow unknown

rootCause: BoardForge previously stopped at an audit result instead of installing/detecting a real backend and separating router availability from DSN/SES workflow availability.
fixSummary: Detect Java, FreeRouting, and DSN/SES workflow independently; select automated routing only when all are present, otherwise generate a manual GUI bridge.
recipe: Probe Java.
Probe FreeRouting.
Probe DSN/SES export/import.
Select automated workflow only when all prerequisites are satisfied.

symptoms: java not on PATH
FreeRouting JAR class version newer than Java runtime

rootCause: FreeRouting v2.2.4 is built with Java 25 and fails under Java 21 with UnsupportedClassVersionError.
fixSummary: Install a portable Java 25 runtime in project tools and run FreeRouting with that explicit java.exe path.
recipe: Install portable Java 25.
Run java -jar freerouting.jar with the Java 25 binary.
Treat Java 21 class-version failure as a runtime mismatch, not router failure.

safetyRules: Do not claim automated import without a verified API

symptoms: KiCad CLI export lacks DSN
KiCad CLI import lacks SES

rootCause: Installed KiCad 10 CLI exposes PCB export/import commands but not Specctra DSN export or Specctra Session import.
fixSummary: Detect CLI/Python/GUI DSN/SES paths separately and use the KiCad GUI manual bridge when CLI automation is unavailable.
recipe: Inspect KiCad CLI export/import help.
Inspect Python API if available.
If GUI is available, generate exact manual Specctra DSN/SES bridge steps.

safetyRules: Verify original spec and unconnected reduction after import

symptoms: FreeRouting runnable
KiCad CLI cannot export/import DSN/SES

rootCause: A real autorouter is installed, but KiCad automation cannot hand off DSN/SES from the command line on this machine.
fixSummary: Generate a precise manual KiCad PCB Editor workflow plus exact FreeRouting command using project-local Java and JAR paths.
recipe: Export Specctra DSN from KiCad PCB Editor.
Run FreeRouting with -de input.dsn -do output.ses.
Import Specctra Session in KiCad PCB Editor.
Run BoardForge DRC/ERC validation.

fixSummary: Import FreeRouting SES network_out directly into KiCad PCB tracks/vias when GUI SES import is unreliable.
Normalize imported FreeRouting track widths to KiCad board minimum while preserving route connectivity.
Normalize imported FreeRouting through vias to legal KiCad diameter and drill constraints.
Classify remaining imported-route clearance and copper-edge issues for local reroute/nudge repair.
After route-dimension normalization, rerun DRC and use remaining unconnected/clearance data for second-pass routing or local repair.
Cluster post-FreeRouting clearance DRC by family, area, net, and offending route object before local reroute/nudge.
safetyRules: Do not discard imported routing globally

fixSummary: Classify hole-clearance clusters into via relocation, segment dogleg, or footprint-hole review before mutation.
safetyRules: Do not discard imported routing globally

fixSummary: Cluster copper-edge violations and repair only by moving imported copper inward, never by changing Edge.Cuts.
safetyRules: Do not discard imported routing globally

fixSummary: Classify solder-mask bridge issues as route-owned or footprint-review; do not edit footprints during post-route cleanup.
safetyRules: Do not discard imported routing globally

fixSummary: Transactional dogleg mutation of imported route segment, accepted only when KiCad DRC decreases without unconnected regression.
Directed dogleg/nudge away from Edge.Cuts for imported copper-edge clusters.
Replace one offending imported segment with same-net dogleg segments while preserving endpoints.

symptoms: FreeRouting through vias violate hole clearance
random doglegs do not repair via-owned DRC

rootCause: Post-FreeRouting repair lacked a legal through-via site search and could not move vias while preserving attached route connectivity.
fixSummary: Build a post-route legal-site map, rank nearby legal through-via locations, relocate imported vias, update attached segment endpoints, and promote only DRC-improving mutations.

symptoms: generic doglegs roll back repeatedly
segment repairs need scored legal waypoints

rootCause: Dogleg generation guessed offsets without proving candidate waypoint legality against board, pad, via, and route obstacles.
fixSummary: Generate and rank legal dogleg waypoints using the post-route obstacle map before rerouting imported segments.

symptoms: classification-only passes
local mutations retained only by segment count

rootCause: Post-route cleanup needed a candidate promotion gate based on KiCad DRC family/total reduction, not mutation existence.
fixSummary: Run candidate mutations on temporary board files, rerun KiCad DRC, and promote only mutations that reduce the target family or total DRC.

symptoms: hole clearance improves while shorting worsens
track crossing appears after via relocation
solder mask bridge count increases after local repair

rootCause: Post-FreeRouting repair promotion accepted target-family reductions without a weighted multi-family DRC score or critical-family regression guard.
fixSummary: Add weighted DRC health scoring, before/after comparison, critical-family collateral damage detection, and rollback of repairs that worsen shorts, crossings, unconnected, forbidden vias, outline, mounting holes, or part/footprint/package state.
recipe: Score DRC before and after every candidate repair
Reject any candidate that worsens shorting, track crossing, unconnected, forbidden via, outline, mounting holes, or part/footprint/package families
Promote only weighted-score improvements with no critical-family regression
Rollback candidate geometry when collateral damage appears
safetyRules: Do not allow shorting or track crossing regression
Do not modify locked board spec

symptoms: guarded repair batch spends all candidates on copper edge clusters
no repairs committed despite legal-site engine
other DRC families never attempted

rootCause: Post-route repair scheduler selected the first prioritized family repeatedly, starving hole-clearance and clearance clusters that had promotable legal-site candidates.
fixSummary: Rotate repair families under the collateral-damage promotion gate so copper-edge, hole-clearance, clearance, solder-mask, and dangling-track clusters all get candidate budgets.
recipe: Run family-rotated candidate selection instead of exhausting one family
Score every candidate with shouldPromotePostRouteRepair
Commit only weighted-score improvements with no critical regression
Continue to next family when all candidates are rejected
safetyRules: Do not trade hole clearance for shorts/crossings
Do not change locked board spec
Do not commit generated PCB artifacts

symptoms: post-route summary flagged forbidden via text hit
hit came from footprint description URL containing microchip.com
actual KiCad via blocks remained standard through vias

rootCause: The post-route summary scanned the whole PCB text for blind/buried/micro terms instead of scanning only KiCad via blocks.
fixSummary: Scan forbidden via terms only inside KiCad via S-expression blocks and ignore footprint descriptions, URLs, and documentation text.
recipe: Parse KiCad PCB text by S-expression via blocks
Detect blind, buried, microvia, or via-in-pad terms only inside via blocks
Ignore footprint descriptions and documentation URLs
Use this scan before promotion and final reporting
safetyRules: Do not weaken forbidden via policy
Do not count documentation text as geometry
Do not promote a route if a real via block uses forbidden technology

before.falsePositive: microchip.com footprint description counted as forbidden via hit

tests: post-FreeRouting forbidden via scan ignores footprint descriptions

symptoms: post-route repair candidate yield was too low and full KiCad DRC was spent on low-probability candidates

rootCause: BoardForge generated legal-looking route mutations without predicting collateral or target-family improvement before expensive DRC.
fixSummary: Add preScorePostRouteRepairCandidate and predicted DRC deltas to reject bad candidates before full KiCad DRC.
recipe: Rank clusters by repairability
Generate candidate mutations
Pre-score target-family improvement and collateral risk
Run full KiCad DRC only for pre-score-approved candidates
Promote only through the multi-family DRC gate
safetyRules: Do not replace full KiCad DRC with pre-score
Do not modify locked board spec

symptoms: guarded repair batches spent candidate budget on low-yield/review-only clusters

rootCause: Cluster selection did not rank route-generated clusters by expected safe repairability.
fixSummary: Add high-yield cluster selection that prefers safe route-generated hole, clearance, and copper-edge clusters and defers review-only clusters.
recipe: Rank clusters by repairability
Generate candidate mutations
Pre-score target-family improvement and collateral risk
Run full KiCad DRC only for pre-score-approved candidates
Promote only through the multi-family DRC gate
safetyRules: Do not replace full KiCad DRC with pre-score
Do not modify locked board spec

symptoms: repair scheduler needed a reusable score for selecting clusters before mutation

rootCause: Clusters were processed mostly by family order instead of repairability evidence.
fixSummary: Rank clusters using family priority, auto-repair safety, density penalty, and review-only penalty before candidate generation.
recipe: Rank clusters by repairability
Generate candidate mutations
Pre-score target-family improvement and collateral risk
Run full KiCad DRC only for pre-score-approved candidates
Promote only through the multi-family DRC gate
safetyRules: Do not replace full KiCad DRC with pre-score
Do not modify locked board spec

symptoms: Corrected pre-score needed to scale beyond a small proof pass on real post-FreeRouting ESC DRC clusters.

rootCause: The previous capped pass validated pre-score correctness but only attempted 4 clusters and 10 full DRC candidates.
fixSummary: Scale high-yield pre-scored repair over more clusters while keeping full KiCad DRC and collateral-damage promotion gate as the final acceptance rule.
recipe: Run high-yield cluster selection
Pre-score mutation candidates
Run full KiCad DRC only on selected candidates
Promote only through collateral guard
Trigger exact-ratsnest pass only after stable DRC improvement
safetyRules: Do not attack unconnected while critical DRC families are unstable

symptoms: Large post-route DRC repair passes need checkpoints so good repairs are not lost during long KiCad DRC batches.

rootCause: Full-board KiCad DRC is expensive and long repair batches can timeout or be interrupted.
fixSummary: Checkpoint the PCB every 5 committed guarded repairs and retain the current work board separately from candidate boards.
recipe: Run high-yield cluster selection
Pre-score mutation candidates
Run full KiCad DRC only on selected candidates
Promote only through collateral guard
Trigger exact-ratsnest pass only after stable DRC improvement
safetyRules: Do not attack unconnected while critical DRC families are unstable

symptoms: Remaining unconnected routing should only start after post-route DRC is trending safely downward.

rootCause: Routing new ratsnest items into a high-DRC board can trade connectivity for shorts, crossings, or clearance debt.
fixSummary: Trigger unconnected routing only after weighted DRC improves, critical families stay stable, forbidden vias remain zero, and at least 10 DRC repairs commit.
recipe: Run high-yield cluster selection
Pre-score mutation candidates
Run full KiCad DRC only on selected candidates
Promote only through collateral guard
Trigger exact-ratsnest pass only after stable DRC improvement
safetyRules: Do not attack unconnected while critical DRC families are unstable

evidence.reason: trigger reached after cleanup9; exact-ratsnest writer queued for next autonomous stage

symptoms: FreeRouting leaves exact KiCad unconnected items after DRC repair
New routes must not trade connectivity for shorts or crossings

rootCause: Remaining post-route ratsnest work needs the same multi-family promotion guard as DRC cleanup.
fixSummary: Rank exact KiCad unconnected items and commit route candidates only when exact connectivity improves without weighted DRC or critical-family regression.
recipe: Parse exact KiCad unconnected items
Rank easy signal, rail, gate, and bootstrap pairs first
Write candidate route transactionally
Diff unconnected item sets
Promote only when exact connectivity improves and weighted DRC does not worsen
safetyRules: Do not commit routes without exact KiCad connectivity proof
Do not worsen shorting, crossings, or unconnected count

symptoms: BoardForge reported internal next actions instead of diagnosing, fixing, testing, and resuming

rootCause: Routing and repair tools existed, but no persistent supervisor converted blockers into internal work plans.
fixSummary: Add a self-driving supervisor facade that diagnoses blockers, ranks next autonomous work, avoids user prompts, and resumes exact-ratsnest or guarded DRC repair.
recipe: Scan current DRC/ERC/unconnected state
Load applicable solutions
Diagnose blockers into module-level work plans
Run the next safe autonomous action
Ask user only for forbidden design decisions
safetyRules: Never ask for another prompt for internal engineering work
Require explicit user approval for forbidden changes

symptoms: Guarded exact-ratsnest writer ranked easy items but blocked before writing copper
writeCandidate adapter was missing from supervisor handoff

rootCause: The guarded ratsnest gate had parser, ranking, and promotion logic, but no physical KiCad writer adapter.
fixSummary: Attach a physical post-route route-bundle writer that writes candidate copper to a branch file, runs DRC when available, and promotes only through the exact-ratsnest DRC gate.
recipe: Build a local route bundle for the exact source/target pads
Write the bundle to a candidate board while preserving imported FreeRouting copper
Run KiCad DRC on the candidate
Promote only if exact connectivity improves and critical DRC families do not worsen
Rollback candidate copper when DRC collateral appears
safetyRules: Do not retain physical routes that introduce shorts or track crossings
Do not use forbidden via types
Do not change board outline, mounting holes, parts, footprints, packages, pads, or nets
Do not push candidate board files to Git

after.reason: candidate routes resolved exact items transiently but failed collateral DRC guard

symptoms: Exact ratsnest writer can transiently resolve items but creates shorting or track-crossing collateral
Post-FreeRouting board has existing shorting_items that make new route promotion unsafe

rootCause: Remaining FreeRouting-imported shorts made the post-route board too unstable for additional exact-ratsnest writes; the supervisor needed to prioritize route-generated short cleanup first.
fixSummary: Pause exact-ratsnest writes while shorts remain, extract route-owned short candidates, remove or reroute only the exact offending generated route object transactionally, and promote only if shorts or weighted DRC improve without critical-family regression.

conditions: imported route copper must be preserved transactionally

recipe: Run KiCad DRC and count shorting_items before attempting new ratsnest writes.
Pause exact-ratsnest writer while route-generated shorts remain.
Extract shorting_items whose offending object is an imported segment or via with a UUID.
Apply one transactional mutation to the exact route object only.
Run KiCad DRC on the candidate board.
Promote only if shorts decrease or weighted DRC improves and no critical family worsens.
Rollback the single candidate if the promotion gate fails.
safetyRules: Do not remove unrelated FreeRouting copper
Do not keep mutations that increase shorts, track crossings, forbidden vias, or unconnected count beyond the configured bound
Do not change board outline, mounting holes, parts, footprints, packages, pads, or nets
Do not push candidate board files to Git

after.mode: shorts-first cleanup enabled before new ratsnest writes

symptoms: BoardForge completed one cleanup stage and returned a next action instead of continuing
Short cleanup created reconnect work that should be scheduled internally

rootCause: Post-route cleanup lacked a persistent state machine that moves from shorts cleanup to short-fix disconnect reroute, DRC cleanup, exact-ratsnest reduction, ERC repair, and export review without user babysitting.
fixSummary: Add a post-route autonomous supervisor state machine that selects the next allowed action, writes resume state, suppresses mid-loop prompt states, and asks the user only when a forbidden design change is required.
recipe: Scan current DRC/ERC and unconnected state.
If shorts exist, run shorts-first cleanup.
If short cleanup created disconnects, reroute those exact disconnects before generic ratsnest work.
If severe generated DRC remains, run guarded local DRC cleanup.
If exact unconnected items remain and critical families are stable, run guarded exact-ratsnest reduction.
Write supervisor resume state after every stage.
Suppress mid-loop user prompt final states unless a forbidden change is required.
safetyRules: Do not ask the user for internal next actions
Do not reintroduce shorts or track crossings
Do not change board outline, mounting holes, parts, footprints, packages, pads, or nets
Do not push board artifacts to Git

before.cleanup12: shorts fixed, next action required short-fix disconnect reroute

symptoms: short cleanup removes unsafe segments and creates exact disconnects
some reconnect candidates reintroduce shorts or fail promotion
supervisor can loop on exhausted disconnects instead of moving to next safe cleanup stage

rootCause: Short-fix disconnect rerouting needed a blocked-with-proof state so exhausted unsafe reconnects do not keep the autonomous post-route supervisor stuck on one stage.
fixSummary: Treat resolved or blocked short-fix disconnects as handled, preserve shorts at zero, and continue automatically to generated-route DRC cleanup when remaining reconnects cannot pass the promotion gate.
recipe: Remove or reroute shorting generated copper first
Diff unconnected items created by short cleanup
Attempt exact short-safe reconnects under the promotion gate
Commit only if shorts remain zero and weighted DRC improves
Mark exhausted reconnects blocked with exact proof
Continue to the next autonomous DRC cleanup stage without user prompting
safetyRules: Do not change board outline or mounting holes
Do not change parts, footprints, packages, pads, or nets
Do not loop forever on blocked reconnects

symptoms: nextStage selected but not executed
esc_postroute_cleaned_continue_autonomously used as final state
user must provide another prompt for allowed internal cleanup stage

rootCause: The post-route supervisor treated selected internal stages as valid user-facing stopping points instead of executing them in a daemon loop.
fixSummary: Add a daemon supervisor contract that executes queued stages, suppresses mid-loop final states, writes runtime checkpoints only at real execution boundaries, and rejects continue_autonomously as a valid final state.
recipe: Scan current board and choose the next internal stage
Execute the selected stage immediately
Write checkpoint after the stage
Re-scan DRC/ERC and select the next stage
Continue until ready, exact blockers, forbidden user decision, or runtime checkpoint
safetyRules: Do not expose queued internal actions as final state
Rollback candidates that worsen weighted DRC or critical families
Never request user input for allowed internal repair work

rootCause: The daemon supervisor marked runtime checkpoint after a zero-commit stage but pointed resume back to the same exhausted stage, allowing an infinite no-progress loop.
fixSummary: Detect zero-commit/no-improvement stages, mark them TEMP_EXHAUSTED_NO_PROGRESS for the current run, skip them in the stage selector, select the next allowed family, and write an executable npm resume command instead of a stage label.

conditions: weighted DRC and unconnected did not improve

recipe: After every daemon stage, compare attempts, commits, weighted DRC, and unconnected deltas
If attempts > 0 and commits = 0 with no retained improvement, mark the stage TEMP_EXHAUSTED_NO_PROGRESS
Add the stage to exhaustedStagesThisRun
Re-run stage selection while skipping exhausted stages for this invocation
Write an executable npm resume command with the board path and --resume
safetyRules: Do not permanently disable the stage; other geometry changes may make it repairable later
Do not resume to a naked stage label
Do not accept a zero-commit stage as progress
Keep shorts, forbidden vias, outline, holes, parts, footprints, packages, pads, and nets protected

rootCause: BoardForge treated a stage boundary or resume-state write as a runtime checkpoint even when wall-clock/tool budget was still available, causing the daemon to stop after selecting the next internal stage.
fixSummary: Add explicit runtime-budget checks and execute the selected next stage when budget remains. Runtime checkpoint is only valid when budget is actually exhausted, an external process cannot continue synchronously, or the environment blocks execution.

conditions: post-route daemon has selected an allowed next stage
no forbidden user decision is required

recipe: After writing resume state, check actual elapsed runtime against configured budget and reserve
If budget remains and the next stage is allowed, execute the stage immediately
Use runtime_limit_reached_resume_written only when runtime/tool/environment limit is real
Keep checkpoint state as recovery data, not as a normal stopping point
safetyRules: Do not use resume state as a substitute for execution
Do not continue if a forbidden board/spec/via change is required
Maintain original-spec protection and DRC promotion gates across each executed stage

rootCause: BoardForge stopped after a productive post-route cleanup stage committed only a few safe fixes instead of continuing that same productive stage until exhausted, complete, or preempted by a higher-priority issue.
fixSummary: Detect productive stage results and keep the same stage selected when it safely commits improvements, so the daemon continues bounded batches until the stage stops producing progress.

conditions: post-route cleanup stage committed safe repairs
weighted DRC or target DRC family improved

recipe: After each stage, check committed repairs and target-family/weighted-score deltas
If the stage safely improved the board and stageComplete is not true, repeat the same stage
Continue bounded batches until no safe commits are found
Only then mark the stage exhausted and select the next allowed stage
safetyRules: Do not continue a productive stage if shorts, forbidden vias, outline, holes, parts, footprints, packages, pads, or nets regress
Do not treat segment count as progress
Rollback individual candidates that fail the promotion gate

problemSignature.invalidFinalState: cleanup continued; board still not fully traced

rootCause: The daemon returned a progress/status summary while the board was not fully traced and an allowed internal next action was known.
fixSummary: Treat progress-state phrases as invalid internal states. The daemon may only return for ready/export, exact user approval, fatal environment blocker, or true runtime checkpoint.

conditions: no forbidden user decision is required

recipe: Suppress progress-only final states
If a productive stage remains, execute it
If a cleanup stage exhausts, select and execute the next stage
Return only on true final state or true runtime/environment checkpoint
safetyRules: Do not hide true runtime limits
Do not continue through forbidden board/spec/via changes
Do not count progress summaries as completion

before.state: cleanup continued; board still not fully traced

rootCause: The post-route supervisor CLI selected the next stage and wrote resume state, but did not call stage executors, so autonomous cleanup still required user babysitting.
fixSummary: Add a daemon CLI loop with a stage executor map. The CLI now scans DRC, selects a stage, executes the mapped worker, checkpoints, and repeats until a valid final state or true runtime checkpoint.

conditions: post-route supervisor has selected an internal stage
no forbidden user decision is required

recipe: Run KiCad DRC on latest board
Select the next post-route stage
Dispatch to a stage executor instead of returning
Write checkpoint after execution
Continue until valid final state or true runtime budget checkpoint
safetyRules: Do not commit board files to plugin repo
Do not promote candidates that introduce shorts, forbidden vias, or original-spec changes
Do not use selector-only resume as a final state

rootCause: The post-route exact-ratsnest CLI stage inherited a tiny generic stage budget, allowing three route attempts to be reported as a runtime checkpoint even though the board still had hundreds of unconnected items.
fixSummary: Separate exact-ratsnest batch controls from generic stage budget. Default ESC post-route ratsnest reduction now selects 100 items, requires a 50-attempt real batch unless fewer are routeable, and records measured runtime before using runtime checkpoint state.
recipe: Use --exact-ratsnest-items default 100
Use --min-ratsnest-attempts default 50
Do not derive ratsnest item count from a smoke-test stage budget
Only report runtime checkpoint when measured runtime budget is exhausted
If zero commits after a real batch, report blocker categories instead of claiming progress
safetyRules: Original board outline, holes, parts, footprints, packages, nets, and pads must remain unchanged

rootCause: BoardForge treated one productive exact-ratsnest batch as a user-facing stop even though unconnected items remained and runtime had not expired.
fixSummary: Carry a productive exact-ratsnest stage forward in the daemon loop. If a batch commits routes or reduces unconnected items, immediately run another real batch unless safety issues, exhaustion, final routing, or real runtime limit stops it.

conditions: exact-ratsnest batch committed one or more routes

recipe: Detect productive exact-ratsnest result
Keep selected stage as run_guarded_exact_ratsnest_reduction for the next daemon loop
Run another 100-item batch
Stop only on exhaustion, safety regression, no unconnected items, or true runtime limit
safetyRules: No board outline, mounting hole, part, footprint, package, pad, or net changes
